DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-18-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. STEPHEN C. UBL of Lewis, Brisbois, Bisgaard & Smith, LLP, Tel No. (415) 362-2580 appearing for Defendant Braden Partners, L.P. dba Pacific Pulmonary Services;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BRADEN PARTNERS, L.P. DBA PACIFIC PULMONARY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained. As to the cause of action for "Products Liability - Negligence," the demurer is sustained without leave to amend. This cause of action is duplicative of the negligence cause of action. (See Ferrari v. Grand Canyon Dories (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 248, 257, "product liability negligence theory is encompassed in plaintiff's separate count for negligence.") As to the cause of action for "Products Liability - Warranty," the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The complaint does not adequately allege facts establishing that any affirmation was part of the basis of the bargain. (See Hauter v. Zogarts (1975) 14 Cal.3d 104, 115.) Additionally, Plaintiffs should allege facts clarifying whether Defendant merely serviced/repaired the equipment, or whether Defendant manufactured and/or sold the equipment. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Sara N. Duncan, Esq. of Sedgwick LLP, Tel. 415.627.1521, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The tentative ruling was uncontested and adopted. DEFENDANT INVACARE CORPORATION'S Motion To Strike Punitive Damages And The Seventh Cause Of Action For Survival Form The Complaint is Granted with 10 days leave to amend. The allegations in the complaint do not support a claim for punitive damages. The following paragraphs are stricken from the complaint: 52, 66, 75, and 82. The following words are stricken from paragraph 100: "and exemplary damages" and "penalties, punitive or exemplary damages, pursuant to California Civil Code sec. 3294." The complaint does not plead facts establishing that Defendant acted with "oppression, fraud, or malice" as required by Civ. Code sec. 3294(a). (See Grieves v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 159, 166, "Not only must there be circumstances of oppression, fraud, or malice, but facts must be alleged in the pleading to support such a claim.") The complaint also does not plead facts establishing an officer, director or managing agent of the corporation "had advance knowledge of the unfitness of the employee and employed him with a conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others or authorized or ratified the wrongful conduct," as required by Civ. Code sec. 3294(b). (See Cruz v. HomeBase (2000) 83 Cal.App.4th 160, 167, "Corporations are legal entities which do not have minds capable of recklessness, wickedness, or intent to injure or deceive. An award of punitive damages against a corporation therefore must rest on the malice of the corporation's employees.") ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Sheryl L. Sawyer CSR# 5976.
